sections.title: Using items on the bank camel

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: What does it look like I'm doing? I'm here to help cleanse the Devourer's corruption from this sacred place.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I didn't expect a camel to be capable of standing up to such a strong enemy. Aren't you worried for your safety?

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: Not particularly. We camels are known for our durability in harsh environments. I think you should be more concerned about yourself. You humans seem so much more fragile than many other species.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure I'll be fine, but I appreciate your concern.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did you get all my stuff?

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: I don't have any of your stuff.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yeah, I don't use a bank!

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: Good for you.

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: Don't you understand how banking works? I don't have all of your stuff, I have simply been trained to access the place where it is all stored.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you're a camel. I didn't know camels could train to become bankers.

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: Well there are ghost bankers, gnome bankers, dwarf bankers, goblin bankers and more! Is it really all that surprising that camels can do it too?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose when you put it like that...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, what do you make of all this?

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I mean everything that's been going on here. It seems like a lot to take in.

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: Well, it really was inevitable, which is a shame really. It saddens me to see what has happened to this tomb.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well at least we're fixing it now.

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: We? Are you sure about that? I find you adventurers often have ulterior motives.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean by that?

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: You may say that you are here to fight the corruption. Indeed, there may be some truth to that. However, would you really be here if there wasn't something for you to gain?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I swear, I'm only here to deal with the Devourer's corruption!

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: I hear you speak those words, but I feel no conviction behind them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I prove it to you? What is it that you are looking for?

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: Why do you care what opinions a camel has of you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want everyone to like me.

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: If your only goal in life is to be liked by all, then you'll probably spend the rest of it chasing the impossible.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to prove you wrong.

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: So you only care because my words cut close to the bone? If you truly want to prove me wrong, then it's your outlook that should change.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It seems to be important to you.

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: You think so?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I do, so tell me, what can I do to show you that not all adventurers are the same?

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: Hmm...

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: Every adventurer seems to destroy without any thought as to what they are doing, nor with any intent to restore what was lost.

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: If you can do that, then that would prove to me that you are infact different from the rest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Restore what was lost? What do you mean by that?

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: That's for you to figure out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, you're right, I'm here for the loot.

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: Well, just try not to desecrate this place any more than it already has been.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you ask me to do again?

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: Every adventurer seems to destroy without any thought as to what they are doing, nor with any intent to restore what was lost.

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: If you can do that, then that would prove to me that you are infact different from the rest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you want me to restore what was lost?

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: Indeed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I believe I understand now.

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: Oh? Do tell.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, while us adventurers have been doing our best to fight against the Devourer's corruption, we've been neglecting what has been lost to achieve that goal. Namely, the four guardians we defeated along the way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ba-Ba was a proud baboon who wished for her troop to flourish. To honour her, I made sure that they won't be forgotten by telling their story to the monkeys of Ape Atoll.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Akkha was cruelly denied death by the Devourer, so I gave his ashes to the High Priest of Icthlarin to give him a proper send-off.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Zebak was fooled into leaving his home and confined within the tomb, so I took his fang and buried it on an island surrounded by crocodiles.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Kephri was promised a future for her race, but it was all a lie. I found a scarab egg within some of her dung, and I gave it to the High Priest of Scabaras to try and nurture it.

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: Well well, colour me impressed adventurer. It seems that you do care after all.

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: It's a shame that much of the damage cannot truly be undone, but I know that Het, Apmeken, Crondis and even Scabaras will be grateful for your actions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just wish there was more I could do.

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: We always do, but you have still done more than most.

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: Now, I have something for you. I've been looking for someone worthy enough to pass this on to.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A key? What's this for?

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: I'll leave that as another puzzle for you to solve.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well thanks, I guess... You know, out of all the camels I've met, you're definitely the strangest one.

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: Well, it does take a special kind of camel to master the portal magic used for bank access.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost that key you gave me.

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: And it conveniently found its way back to me. Try not to lose it this time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have a name?

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: My name is my own, adventurer. It's hardly important right now.

lines.speaker: Cam the Camel
lines.text: I suppose you've earned the right to know. My name is Cam the Camel.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, nice to meet you, Cam the Camel. It'll be nice to call you something other than 'the bank camel' from now on.

lines.speaker: Cam the Camel
lines.text: I'm glad I met a kind adventurer such as yourself. I look forward to seeing you continue your diligent work in cleansing this tomb of the Devourer's corruption.

lines.speaker: Cam the Camel
lines.text: As I said, my name is Cam the Camel.

lines.speaker: Cam the Camel
lines.text: I'm glad I met a kind adventurer such as yourself. I look forward to seeing you continue your diligent work in cleansing this tomb of the Devourer's corruption.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you follow me into the tomb?

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: Why would I want to do that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It would be very helpful. Think of all the supplies I'd have access to if you came with me and followed me around like a portable bank.

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: But you don't even use a bank.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yeah! I forgot.

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: Right...

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: What do you take me for? Some kind of pack yak?

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: Besides, if I leave this place then I won't be able to help any other adventurers with their banking needs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to get going.

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: Suit yourself.

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: Bleh, this grain is disgusting! Do you think I am but a simple cow, adventurer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, no, I was just...

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: This is why I eat the grain in that sack over there. It's specially grown out here in the desert. Once harvested, it goes through a preparation process of several years that...

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: ...and that's why this grain is so special. Not to mention so hard to come by.

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: My, this is some good grain, adventurer! Great flavour profile, not too salty and with just a hint of sweetness. It almost reminds me of...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm, yes?

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: Oh, never mind, I was just thinking about something.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I can't stand around here all day. I've got some things I need to take care of.

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: I won't be eating this, but I do like to collect them. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: Quite the banana you have there! Ba-Ba and her baboon troop were always known for finding the biggest of these. It's a shame that their memory will now disappear into history.

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: Ah, the fang of the mighty Zebak. What a cruel fate to be consumed by ever-lasting hunger.

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: Ah, the ashes of Akkha, a great warrior. It's a shame that his soul never got to experience the release of death.

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: Faeces! How lovely. If I'm not mistaken, this dung is from Kephri herself, last of the dawn scarabs. It's a shame that her kind have long been lost to the world.

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: Hey, get your hands off my food! Unless you'd like me to start eating the contents of your bank instead!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Jokes on you! I don't have a bank!

lines.speaker: Bank Camel
lines.text: Well I hope you're very proud of yourself. Your arbitrary restrictions don't make you better than everyone else, you know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, that's harsh.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow! A needle in a hay sack?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait, isn't it supposed to be a stack, not a sack?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And now that I think about it, this sack is full of grain, not hay...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah well, never mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's just grain...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'm not entirely sure what else I expected.
